title: galeopsis tetrahit subsp. parviflora
intro: Galeopsis tetrahit subsp. parviflora, also known as small-flowered hemp-nettle, is a subspecies of the Galeopsis tetrahit plant, belonging to the mint family. This herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Galeopsis tetrahit subsp. parviflora include relieving coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as soothing digestive issues such as diarrhea and stomach cramps. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate its various health benefits.
